body.title: [sprint] route() returns bare None — walk can't tell capability ceiling from source outage
body.intention: I intend that when the walk finds no usable source it can tell WHY — no more-capable model exists (ceiling) vs a source that should serve is down (outage) — so it halts and escalates with the true cause and can retry only when retrying could differ.
body.description:
**Problem:** RulesEngine.route() returns a bare `None` for ALL no-source cases (rules_engine.py:318-319) — it cannot distinguish (a) 'no model handles the required difficulty' (capability ceiling) from (b) 'a source that should serve this is down' (real availability outage). The domain walk therefore also can't tell them apart: it only sees LOOP_AVAILABILITY. This surfaced during T-ds-loop-no-identical-retry-honest-escalate (halt-on-mid-run-wall shipped 196ba7d9) — the LATENT gap: a capability bump 'code'->'design' that finds no local design source degrades into the availability-retry path and would halt on a mislabeled 'availability-exhausted' when the true cause is a capability ceiling (no more-capable model available). Did NOT appear in the observe-run evidence (which was a hop=0 mid-run timeout) so it was correctly left out of that ticket's scope; filing the sibling now.
**Proposed shape:** give route() a typed no-source signal (e.g. return a small result object / raise a typed no-source reason, or an out-param) that distinguishes 'capability-ceiling' (nothing at/above required difficulty) from 'outage' (candidates exist for this difficulty but none currently available). Thread it up so the walk (domains/base.py) reports the true halt cause and can implement exclusion-aware / changed-strategy retry (retry only when re-selection would differ). Verify exact call sites: rules_engine.route() and BaseDomain.select/run.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (route no-source return), unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/base.py (walk consumes the signal). Confirm exact sites before editing.
**Design rules:** CP1 (honest true-cause over a lying label), CP3 (there's always a why), inference-cost-optimizing-router.
**Scope boundary:** IN — a typed ceiling-vs-outage no-source signal from route() consumed by the walk for an honest halt reason and change-aware retry. OUT — the mid-run-wall halt (already shipped), num_ctx, context eviction.
**Test plan:** unit — a registry with only a 'code'-capable source, required_difficulty='design' -> route() signals CEILING (not outage); a 'design'-capable-but-unavailable source -> route() signals OUTAGE. Walk test: a capability bump onto an absent tier halts with a capability/ceiling reason, not 'availability-exhausted'.
**Completion criteria:** route() emits a machine-distinguishable ceiling-vs-outage no-source reason; the walk's halt names the true cause; a test pins both cases.
**Rollback:** git revert.
